title: V60 — The Hoffmann Method

steps.action: Bloom — pour 50ml in a spiral. Swirl gently.
Pour to 150ml slowly in a circular motion.
Pour to 250ml.
Final pour to 300ml. Give a final gentle swirl.
Drawdown complete. Serve immediately.

notes: The swirl at the end levels the bed for an even extraction. Works beautifully with light to medium roasts.

title: French Press — Long Steep

steps.action: Add coffee. Pour all water. Stir briefly to saturate.
Break the crust, skim foam and floating grounds.
Plunge slowly. Pour immediately — do not let it sit.

notes: Skimming the crust dramatically reduces bitterness. Use freshly roasted dark or medium-dark beans for best results.

title: Moka Pot — Classic Stovetop

steps.action: Fill bottom chamber with hot water to the pressure valve.
Add ground coffee to basket — do not tamp. Level and screw on top.
Place on medium-low heat. Keep lid open to watch.
Once coffee begins to flow golden, reduce heat to lowest.
Remove as soon as sputtering begins. Cool bottom under tap. Serve.

notes: Pre-heating the water prevents bitter overextraction from the metal heating slowly. Works best with dark or medium-dark roasts.

title: Clever Dripper — 4-Minute Steep

steps.action: Rinse filter. Add coffee. Pour all water. Stir once.
Place Clever Dripper on cup to trigger valve. Let drain.
Drawdown complete. Remove dripper. Enjoy.

notes: The Clever is incredibly forgiving — great for trying new beans without dialling in extensively. Adjust steep time to taste.

title: South Indian Filter Kaapi

steps.action: Add finely ground coffee to upper chamber. Press the disc on top lightly.
Pour near-boiling water over the disc. Place lid.
Decoction collects in lower chamber. It should be dark and thick.
Add 30-40ml decoction to tumbler. Top with 120ml hot whole milk. Sweeten to taste.
Pour between tumbler (davara) and cup repeatedly to cool and froth.

notes: Authentic filter kaapi uses a coffee-chicory blend (80:20). The slow drip is key — never rush with boiling water. The davara-tumbler pour is the soul of the ritual.
